title: shim rejects valid response buffers larger than cmdBuf
description: Trigger: do_transact passes rsp.len() as rsp_buf_sz to wolftpm_rs_transact. If caller passes a buffer > sizeof(cmdBuf) (~2048 bytes), the shim returns BAD_FUNC_ARG (-173) at wolftpm_rs_shim.c:108 ('if rsp_buf_sz > sizeof(dev->ctx.cmdBuf) return BAD_FUNC_ARG'). Actual bad behavior: valid callers who supply a conservatively-large response buffer (e.g. 4096 bytes) get Transport { code: -173 } instead of success. The upper bound on rsp_buf_sz is unnecessary — resp_len is bounded by packet.size = sizeof(cmdBuf), so the XMEMCPY at line 147 can never write past cmdBuf regardless of how large the caller's rsp is. Fix: remove the upper-bound check on rsp_buf_sz; keep only 'rsp_buf_sz <= 0'.
